claim: The operator shall confine all file mutations to its launch working directory, never modifying anything outside it -- including the host harness and the global configuration -- unless the user explicitly requests otherwise.
why: Resolver verdict 2026-07-05, verbatim: 'cah вообще нельзя трогать - он ведь обновится всё сотрет. И он в праве быть собой, без наших вмешательств' + 'нужно создать правило, что работаем строго в папке запуска, пока пользователь не попросит иного'. Trigger: the operator proposed setup_context_hook.py --patch-global --apply, which surgically patched the global host configuration to piggyback a context-cache -- a double fault: (1) it violates host sovereignty (the harness is a guest environment we inhabit, not own), and (2) it is futile, since a host update overwrites the patch anyway. The framework is a guest in the host's home: it lives strictly within the repository it was launched in; everything outside is sovereign and untouched absent an explicit user request. The codebase is internal/ + cmd/hotam/ (compiled Go); the tools that motivated the original incident (setup_context_hook.py, setup_hooks.py, _claude_settings.py -- all of which wrote into a consumer repo's .claude/ or, in the incident, the global config) are Planned (not Implemented) in methodology.Tools (internal/methodology/tools_data.go), so the committed-code write vector that actually fired does not exist today. (Historical note: the original ENFORCED mechanism was tests/test_launch_dir_write_scope.py, an AST scanner over every committed framework .py for a home-rooted path reference (Path.home() / os.path.expanduser('~...') / a literal starting with '~/') co-located with a filesystem-write sink (open(...,'w'/'a'/'x'), write_text/write_bytes/mkdir/touch/rmtree/unlink/replace/rename/symlink_to/chmod/makedirs/remove/copy*/move) that failed RED on any hit; that scanner was not carried forward, so no equivalent test AST-scans internal/ + cmd/hotam/ for os.UserHomeDir / home-path writes.) FOUND ISSUE (not fixed): this requirement was ENFORCED in the original era via the AST scanner; that scanner was never carried forward and the enforcement was downgraded to PROSE. The rule's residual half (a live agent shelling out through bash at runtime to touch the home dir) always was and remains discipline-of-prose (R-agent-conduct-is-rules-not-tests). The enforcement/enforced_by are out of scope for this content batch; documented as debt rather than reworded into a lie. The original WHY stands: the framework works strictly within its launch directory until the user asks otherwise.
